summary: A Go monorepo project currently undergoing an aggressive, agent-orchestrated test coverage optimization campaign.
concerns: test files named 'coverage_boost' suggest artificial coverage inflation rather than meaningful tests
high volume of agent-generated test directories (GOrchestra/glm-agents) may introduce maintenance burden
potential test brittleness if tests are written solely to hit coverage thresholds
suggestions: audit 'coverage_boost' tests for actual value versus line coverage manipulation
consolidate or clean up redundant agent-generated test directories
ensure assertion and integration tests focus on edge cases and failure modes
establish a testing standard that prioritizes behavior coverage over line coverage
